prompts.prompt: Is renewing the session token only on login sufficient to prevent session fixation attacks in a Go web app?
What specific attack does NOT renewing the session token on logout enable?
When exactly in the login handler should I call RenewToken — before or after setting the authenticatedUserID session value?
Why should session LoadAndSave middleware be on the dynamic chain and not the standard chain in a Go web app?
Does RenewToken destroy the session data (flash messages, form state) or just generate a new session ID?
How do I implement flash messages in Go using alexedwards/scs that survive a redirect and are automatically deleted after being read?
How do I verify that the authenticated user still exists in the database on every request without hitting the DB in every handler?
What session cookie settings are required for a production Go web app to prevent session hijacking?
I'm implementing privilege escalation (sudo mode) in a Go web app. Do I need to call RenewToken for this too?
